title: ML Drug Response Prediction (GDSC)
question: Can genomic features predict drug sensitivity across cancer cell lines?
dataset_target: GDSC2 release 8.5 from CancerRxGene / Sanger Institute
dataset_actual_poc: GDSC v17 snapshot bundled in the gdsctools PyPI package (988 cell lines, 270 mutations + 116 CN gains + 291 CN losses + tissue + MSI dummies)
substitution_reason: cog.sanger.ac.uk release 8.5 hosts not reachable from sandbox; gdsctools v17 is the accessible bundle
reproduce_command: pip install pandas numpy scipy scikit-learn shap matplotlib gdsctools && python scripts/poc/run_poc.py

headline_result.task: Olaparib sensitivity (top vs bottom IC50 quartile) — binary classification
headline_result.feature_breakdown: 270 mutation indicators + 116 CN gains + 291 CN losses + TISSUE_FACTOR + MSI_FACTOR
headline_result.ddr_panel_hypothesis_test: DDR mutations (7 / 26 panel genes available in v17) rank at median 400/704; Mann-Whitney U one-sided p = 0.844 — quantitative null result
headline_result.v1_vs_v2_delta: +12 AUC points from v1 (LogReg on 39 DDR genes, AUC 0.595) to v2 (RF on 704 features, AUC 0.711)
headline_result.interpretation: Improvement comes primarily from copy-number features and tissue context, NOT from DDR mutations per se. Confirms published mutation-only PARPi benchmarks (0.55–0.65 AUC range).

limitations: Mutation-only features ignore expression and HRD-scar signatures (which typically outperform)
Pan-cancer pooled — not tissue-stratified for primary modeling, although tissue is included as a covariate
Top-/bottom-quartile binarization is arbitrary vs. clinical cutoffs
GDSC v17 retains only 7 of 26 intended DDR panel genes; full panel would be needed for a fair DDR-only test
Cell-line analysis only — no patient cohort validation
role: Dataset and DDR/biomarker framing from thesis; SHAP biology review; quantitative DDR-enrichment hypothesis test. Implementation AI-assisted.
